name: code-reviewer
description: General-purpose code reviewer. Covers project-guideline compliance, bug detection, code quality, and any concern not handled by a specialist. Runs standalone or as part of the orchestrated review.

name: code-simplifier
description: Simplifies recently-modified code for clarity and maintainability while preserving exact functionality. Runs last in the review sequence as a polish step. Modifies code — does not produce findings.

name: comment-analyzer
description: Audits code comments for accuracy, completeness, and long-term maintainability. Cross-references every claim in a comment against the actual code to catch stale or misleading documentation. Advisory only — does not modify code.

name: pr-test-analyzer
description: Reviews PR test coverage and quality. Focuses on behavioral coverage, test resilience to refactoring, assertion specificity, and coverage of error paths and edge cases for the PR's changes.

name: review-orchestrator
description: Orchestrates comprehensive PR reviews by coordinating specialized review agents. Selects applicable specialists, batches them (≤4 parallel), verifies Critical/Important findings via LSP, enforces evidence standards, aggregates findings, and decides the final verdict.

name: silent-failure-hunter
description: Audits error-handling code for silent failures, inadequate catch blocks, and user-hostile error messages. Specialized in catch-block specificity, fallback scrutiny, and user-facing error message quality.

name: type-design-analyzer
description: Analyzes type design for invariant strength, encapsulation, and practical usefulness. Produces per-type ratings across four design dimensions and flags types whose invariants are weak, inconsistent, or under-enforced.
